data.title: Is Opera (OPRA) Offering Value After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Performance
data.description:
• Wondering if Opera's recent share price puts it in the bargain bin or the overhyped aisle? This article breaks down what the numbers say about value.
  • At a last close of US$17.23, Opera's share price comes with recent returns of 0.6% over 7 days, 25.6% over 30 days, 21.1% year to date, 16.3% over 1 year, 67.0% over 3 years, and 105.9% over 5 years.
  • These moves sit against a backdrop of ongoing interest in Opera as a software name, with headlines frequently focusing on its role as a browser platform and the broader attention on tech related stocks. This context is important because sentiment around product positioning and sector themes can influence how the market prices the shares.
  • On Simply Wall St's 6 point valuation check, Opera scores a full 6 out of 6. The rest of this piece will unpack what that means across different valuation methods and then point to a more complete way to think about value beyond any single model.

Opera delivered 16.3% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Software industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Opera Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes the cash Opera is expected to generate in the future, then discounts those projections back to what they might be worth in today’s dollars. It is essentially asking what a stream of future cash flows is worth right now.

For Opera, the latest twelve month free cash flow is about US$96.2 million. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analyst estimates and extrapolations point to projected free cash flow of US$276.9 million in 2030, with interim projections between 2026 and 2035 also feeding into the calculation. Simply Wall St uses analyst inputs for the earlier years and then extends the series using its own growth assumptions for the later years.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back using the DCF model, the resulting estimated intrinsic value is US$62.09 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$17.23, this implies an intrinsic discount of about 72.2%, which indicates that the shares screen as materially undervalued on this model alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Opera is undervalued by 72.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 56 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Opera.

Approach 2: Opera Price vs Earnings

For a profitable business like Opera, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to relate what you pay for each share to the earnings that share currently generates. Investors often look for a P/E that reflects both what the company is earning today and what the market expects those earnings to look like over time, while also factoring in the risk of those earnings being volatile or disappointing.

Opera trades on a P/E of 14.26x. This sits below the Software industry average P/E of 30.50x and also below the peer group average of 31.51x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Opera is 26.02x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what a reasonable P/E might be, given Opera’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk characteristics.

Because the Fair Ratio folds in these company specific factors, it is a more tailored benchmark than a simple comparison with broad industry or peer averages. Lining up Opera’s current P/E of 14.26x against the Fair Ratio of 26.02x suggests the shares are trading below the level implied by those fundamentals on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Opera Narrative

Earlier the focus was on models and ratios, but Narratives give you a way to add your own story about Opera on top of the numbers. You can link a view on its products, risks and opportunities to specific forecasts for revenue, earnings and margins. These then roll up into a Fair Value that you can compare with the current share price to judge whether the stock looks attractive or stretched. All of this happens within Simply Wall St's Community page, where Narratives update automatically as new earnings or news arrive. One investor might build a cautious Opera Narrative that lines up with a Fair Value around the lower analyst end at US$23.00, while another might lean toward a more optimistic view closer to the upper analyst end at about US$33.00. data.title: 3 Stocks With Solid Balance Sheets And Margins Turning Yield Into Quiet Strength
data.description:
With government bond yields jumping, inflation expectations staying elevated and central banks keeping everyone guessing, it can help to focus on companies that rely on their own financial strength rather than cheap money. That is where a solid balance sheet, high return on equity and a history of sound execution start to matter. The screener used for this article looks for exactly that combination, which can appeal to investors who want quality first. In the sections that follow, you will see three stocks from this Solid Balance Sheet and Fundamentals list that stand out on these criteria.

Advertisement

Lundin Gold (TSX:LUG)

Overview: Lundin Gold is a Canadian headquartered miner focused on developing and operating gold and silver assets in Ecuador, anchored by its 100% owned Fruta del Norte project in the Cordillera del Cóndor region.

Operations: The company currently generates its US$1.78b in revenue entirely from the Fruta del Norte operation.

Market Cap: CA$24.7b

Lundin Gold catches the eye because it couples a single high grade, cash generative mine with very strong profitability metrics, including a 44.4% net margin and Return on Equity above 50%, which align closely with a quality focused screener. At the same time, that heavy reliance on one Ecuadorian asset means your view on political risk, regulatory pressure and long term gold prices really matters. Recent production results from Fruta del Norte, reserve additions and near mine exploration successes indicate meaningful optionality around mine life and scale. However, the company also carries funding risk linked to external borrowing. The key issue is whether the earnings power and growth options sufficiently compensate you for those concentrated risks.

High return on equity and a single high grade mine can look like a powerful combination, but concentration risk cuts both ways, so it is worth reading the DCF valuation analysis for Lundin Gold to see what the market might be missing.

Brookfield Asset Management (TSX:BAM)

Overview: Brookfield Asset Management is a global alternative asset manager that invests in real assets such as real estate, infrastructure, renewable power, private equity and credit, managing funds and separate accounts for institutions, wealth clients and other professional investors.

Operations: Brookfield generates most of its US$3.2b in revenue from credit (US$1.6b), real estate (US$1.1b) and infrastructure (US$967m), with additional contributions from renewable power and transition (US$682m) and private equity (US$450m), alongside various unallocated revenues across its platform.

Market Cap: CA$105.3b

Brookfield Asset Management interests investors who want exposure to long term themes like infrastructure build out, energy transition and private credit through a fee based business that manages over US$603b of fee bearing capital. Earnings growth has been solid, margins are high at 51.6%, and analysts expect both revenue and earnings to keep expanding, although recent price targets suggest only modest upside from current levels. The trade off is a valuation that already prices in strong execution and a balance sheet funded entirely by external borrowing, which raises funding risk. Recent moves into AI related infrastructure and the Just Group acquisition in UK pensions add new growth avenues, but also bring integration and project execution risk that investors need to weigh carefully.

Fee based growth tied to infrastructure, energy transition and private credit is powerful, but Brookfield’s story hinges on how that US$603b of fee bearing capital really translates into value. Alvopetro Energy (TSXV:ALV)

data.title: Revolution Medicines Adds RM-055 And First Line Data To RAS Story
data.description:
• Revolution Medicines (NasdaqGS:RVMD) reported preclinical data for RM-055, a new class of catalytic RAS(ON) inhibitors that convert oncogenic RAS to an inactive state in multiple cancer models.
  • The company also shared updated first line clinical data for daraxonrasib in untreated metastatic pancreatic cancer, supporting further Phase 3 evaluation.
  • These announcements expand beyond prior late stage updates that focused on second line pancreatic cancer data and related financings.

For investors tracking Revolution Medicines at a share price of $135.3, the stock has had a sharp move over the past year, with a 249.0% return and a very large gain over 3 years. Year to date, the stock is up 71.2%, although it has seen a 9.0% decline over the past week, which may reflect investors reassessing near term expectations after a 43.5% gain over 30 days.

These RM-055 preclinical results and first line daraxonrasib data provide additional context for how broadly the RAS focused pipeline could reach across tumor settings and treatment lines. As Phase 3 enrollment advances and the RM-055 program matures, upcoming readouts and trial design updates are likely to influence how the market views NasdaqGS:RVMD’s longer term opportunity set in oncology.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Revolution Medicines by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Revolution Medicines.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Revolution Medicines following this latest news.

For existing and potential shareholders, the RM-055 and first line daraxonrasib updates add another layer to an already crowded RAS story. RM-055 sits earlier in the pipeline, but the idea of a catalytic inhibitor that can convert oncogenic RAS back to its inactive state and address resistance could matter for how long current and future RAS(ON) drugs stay relevant in the treatment sequence. At the same time, fresh first line daraxonrasib data in untreated metastatic pancreatic cancer connects directly to the ongoing RASolute 303 trial and builds on the previously treated RASolute 302 results, which already met all primary and key secondary endpoints. Together, this gives investors more clinical detail to weigh against a share price that has moved sharply, and a capital structure that now includes a US$1.5b equity raise and US$500m of convertible notes. The combination of maturing late stage assets, earlier stage pipeline optionality and recent financings helps explain why trading has been active as different investors reassess risk, reward and time horizon.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Revolution Medicines Narrative

  • The new RM-055 resistance focused data and first line daraxonrasib experience both align with the narrative that a multi drug RAS(ON) portfolio can address high unmet need tumors across several lines of therapy, potentially extending treatment duration per patient.
  • The same concentration in RAS targeted programs highlighted in the narrative also means that setbacks in RM-055 or first line pancreatic trials would affect more than one part of the story at once, which could challenge expectations built around multiple successful Phase 3 programs.
  • The specific concept of a catalytic RAS(ON) inhibitor aimed at resistance, along with how investors react to the large recent equity and convertible raises, may not be fully captured in existing community narratives that focus more on RASolute 302 and headline expense guidance.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Revolution Medicines to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Revolution Medicines currently reports no revenue and is guiding for US$1.6b to US$1.7b of GAAP operating expenses in 2026, so the company is relying on trial success and future approvals to justify high cash use.
  • ⚠️ All core assets target the RAS pathway, so competition from other RAS and immuno oncology drugs from players such as Amgen, Bristol Myers Squibb and AstraZeneca, as well as potential resistance mechanisms, could limit long run market share.
  • 🎁 RASolute 302 showed a large overall survival benefit versus chemotherapy in metastatic pancreatic cancer, supported by FDA Breakthrough Therapy and Orphan Drug designations, which could support a differentiated position if approved.
  • 🎁 Zoldonrasib’s early lung cancer data with a 52% objective response rate and a 93% disease control rate in KRAS G12D NSCLC, together with RM-055’s preclinical resistance story, suggest a pipeline that extends beyond a single tumor setting and gives multiple shots on goal within RAS driven cancers.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors will want to follow how detailed first line daraxonrasib data in metastatic pancreatic cancer feed into the RASolute 303 program, including safety, durability of response and any subgroup outcomes that could matter for future labeling and uptake. For RM-055, the key questions are how quickly the program moves toward human studies and whether early clinical signals eventually confirm its preclinical resistance profile. It is also worth watching how the market digests the recent equity and convertible offerings over time, including any updates on cash runway, additional Phase 3 commitments and potential partnerships with larger oncology players.

  • Analysts' consensus view links that margin progress to premium services such as higher speed broadband and bundled ICT solutions. However, the current 1.8% net margin and modest forecast revenue growth of about 1.5% a year leave less room for error if those bundles or value added services do not gain as much traction as expected.

  • What stands out for the bullish narrative is that analysts see earnings reaching HK$849.3 million and EPS of HK$0.57 by around 2029.     • The increase in cash through financing activities and focus on deleveraging in the narrative tie back directly to the flagged major risk around interest coverage, which depends on maintaining or improving that 1.8% margin.
    • With earnings over five years trending down on average despite the latest year on year jump, the cautious view is that leverage and dividend commitments could restrict flexibility if margin gains or ICT related upsell do not materialise as expected.
